commit.message:
chore(changelog): record scan safety fix hash

Summary:
- replace the unreleased scan/status fix placeholder with the actual
  implementation hash

Rationale:
- release-note-worthy changes in this repo must carry the concrete short
  hash before handoff

Validation:
- not run; changelog hash update only

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

fix(api): harden scan and simulator status

Summary:
- block Scan.scan/1 from probing a backend that is already owned by a
  live conflicting master session
- infer simulator backend identity from live transport info when the
  runtime was started without a stored backend, and make detached runs
  explicit with backend=nil
- tighten the simulator status contract and add regression coverage for
  backend conflicts, scan safety, and subscription shape

Rationale:
- a standalone scan must not mutate station addresses on the same
  backend a running master already owns
- the new machine-readable status surface should not silently lose
  transport identity or advertise the wrong subscriptions type

Validation:
- mix format
- mix compile --warnings-as-errors
- mix test test/ethercat/backend_test.exs test/ethercat/scan_test.exs test/ethercat/simulator_test.exs
- mix test

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
chore(changelog): record backend refactor hash

Summary:
- replace the unreleased backend/status/scan refactor placeholder with
  the actual implementation commit hash

Rationale:
- the repo requires release-note-worthy changes to carry the concrete
  short hash before handoff

Validation:
- not run; changelog hash update only

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

refactor(api): make backend and runtime roles explicit

Summary:
- add EtherCAT.Backend normalization plus first-class
  EtherCAT.Master.Status, EtherCAT.Simulator.Status, and
  EtherCAT.Scan.Result surfaces
- require explicit backend: startup for master and simulator, and
  update docs, fixtures, hardware helpers, and the capture task to
  use the new transport model
- add status and scan coverage, and migrate simulator and hardware
  support tests to the backend-aware API

Rationale:
- callers should not have to reconstruct whether the simulator,
  master, and observed bus are running from fragmented queries
- explicit backend attachment and topology-first scan results match
  the product model better than the old mixed transport options

Validation:
- mix format
- mix compile --warnings-as-errors
- mix test

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
Publish Pages docs for the OAI RFsim + UE sim dashboard walkthrough

The Pages docs already described the runtime bridge and proof
boundaries, but reviewers still had to reconstruct the simulation
 dashboard path from multiple docs, issues, and artifact directories.
This change adds a dedicated walkthrough page, publishes the current
dashboard screenshot in the docs bundle, and wires the page into the
architecture navigation and cross-references so future issue-driven
refreshes have one place to edit.

Constraint: Published docs must keep simulation proof distinct from
Constraint: bounded-standards and real-lab proof tiers
Rejected: Fold the walkthrough into doc 09 only | harder to keep
Rejected: issue/PR evidence and media reviewable in one place
Confidence: high
Scope-risk: narrow
Reversibility: clean
Directive: Refresh the screenshot asset and issue/PR evidence links
Directive: together whenever dashboard proof media changes
Tested: git diff --check
Tested: npm run docs:build
Tested: Rendered VitePress page and published asset spot checks
Not-tested: Fresh runtime dashboard launch or new YON-101 media capture

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
Surface repo-local OAI UE simulation proof in the dashboard

The repo-local split gNB plus OAI UE request set now carries a committed
observe entrypoint, simulation proof metadata, and dashboard coverage for
attach, registration, session, and ping outcomes. The dashboard also keys
focused runs by artifact identity instead of shared change ID so observe,
verify, and capture-artifacts entries for the same lane stay selectable.

This also checks in reviewer-facing screenshots so the PR can show the
operator path and explicitly mark the captured state as simulation proof,
not real-lab proof.

Constraint: Simulation evidence must remain visibly separate from live-lab claims
Constraint: PR media lives under a gitignored artifacts tree and must be staged intentionally
Rejected: Reuse change_id as the dashboard focus key | observe/verify/capture runs for one lane collide
Confidence: high
Scope-risk: moderate
Reversibility: clean
Directive: Keep run focus keyed by per-artifact identity whenever one lane can emit multiple phase artifacts
Tested: mix contract_ci
Tested: mix runtime_ci
Tested: bin/ran-dashboard plus curl -sf http://127.0.0.1:4050/api/health
Tested: curl -sf http://127.0.0.1:4050/api/dashboard | jq '.activity.recent_changes[] | select(.id=="chg-oai-du-ue-local-001") | {id, phase, protocol_state: .protocol_state.outcome_rows}'
Not-tested: Full live Docker RFsim bringup was not re-run after final staging in this handoff pass

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
fix(mobile-admin): remove LIBRARY_SEARCH_PATHS from iOS hardening plugin (#950)

The xcode npm pbxproj parser cannot re-parse $() variable references
(like $(inherited) and $(SDKROOT)) after writeSync. This broke
downstream Expo mods like withEntitlementsPlist during prebuild.

Expo/RN prebuild already sets LIBRARY_SEARCH_PATHS correctly, so
the plugin should not modify it.

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix(checkout): remove per-route CSRF checks blocking guest storefront checkout (#949)

PR #809 added checkCsrfProtection() to 156 API routes, but incorrectly
included storefront guest-facing routes. Guest users don't have CSRF tokens,
so POST /api/shipping/quotes returns 403 — breaking checkout before orders
can be created. No orders have been recorded since March 18 (10 days).

The Origin-based CSRF middleware in proxy.ts already covers all routes at
the middleware layer, making these per-route checks redundant. Remove them
from 7 guest-facing routes: shipping/quotes, shipping/quote,
discount-codes/validate, forms/submit, storefront/customer, wishlist,
and reviews.

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
refactor(BUB-150): extract shared session-linking helpers (#126)

Summary:
- add a shared session-linking helper module for sibling parent-link
  inference and task window extraction
- rewire Claude and OpenClaw adapters to inject runtime-specific transcript
  readers while reusing the shared logic
- add focused session-linking unit coverage and record the reusable pattern in
  progress tracking

Rationale:
- remove duplicated linking logic across the two adapters without changing
  runtime-specific missing-file behavior
- keep adapter isolation by centralizing the core algorithm while preserving
  runtime-owned I/O and error policy decisions

Tests:
- npm run --workspace @bububuger/spanory test -- --run
  test/unit/session-linking.spec.ts test/unit/adapter.spec.ts
  test/unit/openclaw.adapter.spec.ts
- npm run check
- npm test
- npm run test:bdd

commit.message:
Stabilize and expand the Playwright e2e suite

Run Playwright against a dedicated smoke environment instead of reusing an already-running local app. Use an isolated smoke port and explicit smoke database path, seed the e2e user before startup, and keep the managed Playwright server path separate from intentionally external base URLs.

Expand coverage beyond the old login smoke check to cover dashboard rendering and graph toggles, diary create/edit/delete, pain create/edit/delete, settings preference persistence, and password change plus restoration. Exclude chatbot coverage for now because that flow is scheduled for removal.

Add cleanup-first test helpers so dummy e2e data is purged before and after each test run, and keep execution single-worker to avoid cross-test contamination while operating on the same dedicated account.

Closes #53

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
Add DU/CU runtime protocol-state panels to the dashboard

The dashboard snapshot now normalizes repo-local OAI DU/CU protocol
fields and bounded-standards protocol evidence into a documented
operator surface. The inspector replaces the single generic OAI card
with distinct simulation and standards sections, and the snapshot
contract plus architecture notes now carry the provenance needed to
explain each field and counter.

Constraint: Must keep repo-local simulation proof visually distinct from bounded-standards evidence
Constraint: No new dependencies for the dashboard surface or validation path
Rejected: Leave protocol proof as a generic token list | operators still need raw-log parsing and source hunting
Confidence: high
Scope-risk: moderate
Reversibility: clean
Directive: Extend protocol-state panels only from declared observe/verify artifact contracts so the UI does not invent protocol claims
Tested: mix test apps/ran_observability/test/ran_observability/dashboard_snapshot_test.exs; bin/ran-dashboard on http://127.0.0.1:4051 with RAN_TOPOLOGY_FILE=config/lab/topology.single_du.rfsim.json; curl /api/health and /api/dashboard; rendered screenshot artifacts/pr_media/yon-98/dashboard-protocol-state-tall.png
Not-tested: GitHub Actions/PR checks
Related: YON-98

commit.message:
perf(BUB-154): cache regex compilation and backfill concurrency (#123)

Summary:
- memoize secret-pattern and capture wildcard regex compilation
- skip repeated session mkdir calls and add bounded backfill concurrency
- add regression tests for caching, mkdir dedupe, and concurrency helpers

Rationale:
- reduce repeated hot-path work in redaction, capture, and capture-store
- speed backfill throughput while preserving per-session error isolation
- lock the perf wins behind focused unit coverage and full repo validation

Tests:
- npm --workspace @bububuger/spanory exec vitest run
  test/unit/redaction.spec.ts test/unit/capture.spec.ts
  test/unit/capture-store.spec.ts test/unit/commands.backfill.spec.ts
- npm run check && npm test && npm run test:bdd

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
refactor(BUB-149): centralize shared defaults and helpers (#122)

Summary:
- move shared default thresholds and REDACTED token into
  @bububuger/core and point CLI/plugin consumers at that source
- switch duplicated clamp helpers to the core export and add a
  thin CLI constants re-export for internal call sites
- replace runtime/setup HOME fallbacks with resolveUserHome() and
  sync the statusline workspace dependency in package-lock.json

Rationale:
- this ticket targets cross-package dedup, so a CLI-local constants
  file was not a sufficient canonical source
- keeping shared defaults in core removes drift between core, CLI,
  statusline, and opencode plugin behavior without changing APIs
- HOME resolution now follows one helper path, reducing repeated
  env fallback logic across runtime entrypoints

Tests:
- npm run check
- npm test
- npm run test:bdd

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
feat: add runtime-qualified type semantics (#26)

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai
-->
## Summary by CodeRabbit

* **New Features**
* Surface `runtime` syntax is normalized into runtime-tagged return
types and propagates through expressions, operators, borrowing, and
control flow; function headers no longer render a separate `runtime`
prefix.

* **Bug Fixes**
* Demoting from `runtime T` to `T` is a hard error; joins promote to
`runtime`; compatibility now uses structural shape rules.

* **Documentation**
* Clarified runtime-tag semantics, directional conversion, and
normalization of `runtime fn` sugar.

* **Tests**
* Expanded unit and e2e tests for promotion, demotion errors, joins,
extern returns, printing, and lowering.
<!-- end of auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

---------

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
feat(jumia): push Baci stock levels to Jumia (#946)

* feat(jumia): push Baci stock levels to Jumia with manual + auto sync

Add one-way stock sync from Baci to Jumia. Merchants can manually push
stock via a new "Sync Stock" button in the channels dashboard, and stock
also auto-syncs every 5 minutes piggybacked on the order sync cron when
sync_config.stock is enabled.

- DB migration: add baci_stock_at_last_sync and last_stock_synced_at columns
- API route: POST /api/marketplace/jumia/products/stock with batched queries
- UI: Sync Stock button + syncStock() hook function
- Edge function: piggyback stock push after order sync (gated by sync_config)
- Tests: 10 route tests covering auth, validation, stock change detection

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(jumia): apply CodeRabbit review fixes for stock sync + add negotiation push notifications

- Edge function: replace Promise.allSettled with Promise.all for Supabase error detection, extract duplicate stockPayload
- Stock route test: add variant-level mapping test exercising mockVariantsIn path
- syncStock: align error handling with syncOrders (include data.details)
- client-page: add void prefix to fire-and-forget refetch() calls
- expo-push: add notifyNegotiationRequest and notifyNegotiationResponse functions
- webhook: fix notification amount to include shipping_fee in chat order total

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: apply CodeRabbit round 3 — error fallbacks, consistent formatting, test coverage

- syncStock/syncOrders: guard against undefined data.error when data.details is present
- notifyNegotiationRequest: use 'orders' channel for high-priority delivery
- notifyNegotiationRequest: use consistent Intl.NumberFormat for currentPrice
- expo-push tests: add error handling (Supabase error, Expo API failure, token invalidation)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor(expo-push): extract shared formatCurrency helper, strengthen test assertions

- Extract module-level formatCurrency() replacing 8 inline Intl.NumberFormat calls
- Assert token passed to .in() in DeviceNotRegistered test for comprehensive coverage
- Add error handling tests: Supabase query error, Expo API failure
- Guard syncOrders/syncStock against undefined data.error when details present

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(expo-push): guard against division by zero in notifyPriceDrop

Prevents "Infinity% Off!" title when oldPrice is 0.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(deps): bump path-to-regexp override to >=8.4.0 (fixes 2 Dependabot alerts)

Resolves ReDoS vulnerabilities via sequential optional groups (high)
and multiple wildcards (medium) in path-to-regexp <8.4.0.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor(push): extract negotiation notifications to dedicated module

- Move notifyNegotiationRequest/notifyNegotiationResponse to
  negotiation-notifications.ts (one-utility-per-file convention)
- Export formatCurrency from expo-push for reuse
- Fix !currentPrice falsy check → currentPrice == null (0 is valid)
- Guard divide-by-zero in discount calc when currentPrice === 0
- Update all import sites and mocks
- Add 8 colocated tests including zero-price edge cases

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

commit.message:
fix(push): rewrite push notification system with expo-server-sdk (#943)

* fix(push): rewrite push notification system with expo-server-sdk, app_type filtering, and missing triggers

Migrate from manual fetch to expo-server-sdk for token validation, chunking,
and receipt polling. Add app_type column filtering so merchant notifications
only reach admin tokens and customer notifications only reach storefront tokens.
Wire up missing push triggers for POD/invoice/wallet orders, Credit Direct and
Juicyway webhooks, product reviews, and negotiation requests/responses. Add
receipt polling cron, stale token cleanup, and iOS release hardening config plugin.
Remove unused firebase-admin.ts and tracked Firebase config secrets.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(push): address CodeRabbit review findings for push notification system

- Fail-closed CRON_SECRET check, fetchError → 500, named Expo import in cron route
- Try/catch + error responses in notify-negotiation and negotiations/notify routes
- Scoped query (.eq merchant_id) prevents cross-merchant notification injection
- EXPO_ACCESS_TOKEN warning when missing, surface processTickets storage errors
- LIBRARY_SEARCH_PATHS preservation + APNs env based on EAS build profile
- Fix Notifications race condition with async-ready pattern in storefront hook
- Replace hardcoded merchant_id with prop in NegotiationModal + update 4 callers
- Fix juicyway hardcoded NGN → use order.currency, chat order amount includes shipping
- Add negotiationId param to notifyNegotiationResponse for deep-link support
- Add app_type passthrough assertions in push-tokens/register tests
- Add NegotiationModal failure path test, update all affected test assertions

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(push): address CodeRabbit review round 2 — error handling, type safety, and guards

- Independent try/catch for notifyNewOrder/notifyPaymentReceived in all webhook after() callbacks
- Separate 500 vs 401 for missing INTERNAL_API_SECRET in notify-negotiation endpoint
- Guard NegotiationModal rendering with merchant?.id check (prevents empty-string FK)
- Add merchantId early guard in submitMerchantRequest (prevents DB constraint violation)
- Handle getUser() auth errors in NegotiationModal (log and continue as guest)
- Remove redundant cleanup_old_push_tickets RPC calls (single invocation per cron run)
- Replace any types with proper expo-notifications types in storefront push hook
- Fix LIBRARY_SEARCH_PATHS tokenizer to respect quoted paths with spaces + handle array input
- Await customer notification in admin negotiations (surface errors instead of swallowing)
- Add negotiation deep link support (negotiation_id → detail, productSlug → product page)
- Extract orderNum in orders route to deduplicate 3 identical computations
- Add deactivation error handling in expo-push.ts token cleanup
- Remove dead mock variables in expo-push.test.ts
- Add app_type default assertion in push-tokens register test
- Add missing-secret 500 test for notify-negotiation endpoint
- New test files: CartSidebar.test.tsx, cart.test.tsx (7 tests)

- Add client-side access guards for EXPO_ACCESS_TOKEN and INTERNAL_API_SECRET
- Add missing serverEnv mapping for INTERNAL_API_SECRET and EXPO_ACCESS_TOKEN (critical: getters returned undefined without this)
- Add merchantId early guard in all 3 NegotiationModal variants (web + 2 mobile)

- Timing-safe secret comparison in cron routes (cleanup-push-tokens, push-receipts)
- Chunk failure tracking in receipt polling (returns 207 on partial failure)
- Cleanup RPC error checking with cleaned: true/false response
- Discriminated union schema for notify-negotiation (single requires itemName/currentPrice)

Replace the heavy coder base with a slim Debian image while keeping mise and bun available for runtime provisioning. Install core shell utilities in the image and hydrate developer tools with mise on startup so cold boots stay fast and tool state persists across container instances.

fix(browser): dev-browser recipe overhaul (#98)

* fix(browser): dev-browser recipe overhaul — filechooser upload, strict errors, QuickJS compat

Complete rewrite of the dev-browser ChatGPT recipe after extensive dogfood testing
with GPT-5.4-Pro Extended (470KB source bundle e2e).

Browser connection:
- Fix command_is_available() to use --help (dev-browser doesn't support --version)
- Add npm prefix -g fallback for finding dev-browser when not in PATH
- Auth check uses #prompt-textarea element (text markers drift with UI updates)
- Skip page-level auth check before recipe (interferes with subsequent session)
- Recover stdout from QuickJS GC crash on sandbox disposal

File upload (filechooser flow):
- Replace DataTransfer hack with native filechooser (+ button → menu → setFiles)
- Manual UTF-8 encoder (no TextEncoder/btoa in QuickJS sandbox)
- Buffer.from(new Uint8Array(bytes)) — Buffer.from(string) is base64 in sandbox
- Fixed 5s wait after upload (polling evaluate disrupts React state)

Text input & send:
- keyboard.type() with delay (fill/execCommand bypass ProseMirror React state)
- browser.newPage() per run (named pages accumulate stale ChatGPT state)
- page.evaluate() args for ASSISTANT_COUNT (Playwright runs in browser context)

Error handling (GPT-5.4-Pro review findings):
- Strict check_chatgpt_auth() JSON parsing (no more unwrap_or(json!({})))
- Strict run_chatgpt_recipe() response validation (fail on missing response)
- Lazy bundle text read in main.rs (only in paste mode)
- Model selection 5s timeout + warning-only fallback
- Scoped error detection (querySelector on error elements, not full page body)

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* style: cargo fmt

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
chore(BUB-155): harden CI and package governance (#117)

* chore(BUB-155): harden repo governance config

Summary:
- pin GitHub Actions workflows to approved full SHAs
- add explicit workspace/package metadata for ghost dependency checks
- switch root npm registry config to public npm and add repo governance test

Rationale:
- enforce immutable action refs and explicit package/tooling declarations
- align root publish defaults with the public npm registry
- preserve issue progress while full repo gates remain blocked by baseline build/test instability

Tests:
- pass: cd packages/cli && npx vitest run test/unit/repo-governance.spec.ts --reporter=verbose
- fail: npm test (pre-existing core/otlp-core dist gate instability)
- fail: npm run check (repo-wide check instability under current baseline)

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* fix(BUB-155): export workspace package entrypoints

Summary:
- add root package exports for core, otlp-core, and backend-langfuse
- extend the repo governance test to assert workspace dependency and export
  contracts
- update progress notes with the export follow-up

Rationale:
- keep NodeNext workspace consumers aligned with explicit dist entrypoints
- lock the ghost-dependency hardening work behind an automated regression test

Tests:
- npx vitest run packages/cli/test/unit/repo-governance.spec.ts --config
  packages/cli/vitest.config.ts
- npm exec tsc -- --project packages/cli/tsconfig.runtime.json --noEmit
- npm run --workspace @bububuger/backend-langfuse check

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* fix(BUB-155): export workspace package entrypoints

Summary:
- add explicit root export maps for core, otlp-core, and backend-langfuse
- normalize esbuild entry paths to ./dist/index.js in CLI and plugin builds
- extend repo governance coverage for workspace exports and import-backed deps

Rationale:
- package-name imports need stable dist/type entrypoints across workspace builds
- explicit relative esbuild entry paths reduce resolution ambiguity in build scripts
- keep ghost dependency and package entry guarantees enforced in one repo-level spec

Tests:
- pass: ./node_modules/.bin/vitest run packages/cli/test/unit/repo-governance.spec.ts
- fail: npm run check (pre-existing workspace build instability tracked in BUB-160)
- fail: npm test (pre-existing workspace build instability tracked in BUB-160)

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
feat(chatview): harden pi chat and no-focus desktop automation

Summary:
- extract chat model selection into a dedicated controller and
  centralize setup prompt helpers for cleaner chatview ownership
- preserve non-Pi transcript history across Pi sync, reset fresh-chat
  composer state correctly, and clean up duplicate embeddings status
  bars after reloads
- harden the desktop automation bridge, stable-client bootstrap,
  reload/chatview stress runners, and release preflight privacy and
  gitignore safety checks

Rationale:
- keep the Pi-integrated chatview simpler while fixing the real model
  switch, fresh-chat, and transcript continuity regressions exposed by
  live vault testing
- make no-focus Obsidian automation the canonical release-proof path,
  including reload churn, bridge recovery, and duplicate-status-bar
  detection
- block local-only, secret-like, or missing-ignore release hazards
  before version tagging and release publication

Tests:
- git diff --check
- git check-ignore -v .env.local .codex/diagnostics/check-plugin-fast-debug-20260224-015345.log .codex/diagnostics/check-plugin-fast-debug-20260224-015444.log .codex/diagnostics/check-plugin-fast-verbose-20260224-015427.log
- npm run check:plugin:fast
- npm run build
- npm test
- node --test testing/native/desktop-automation/bootstrap.test.mjs testing/native/desktop-automation/client.test.mjs testing/native/desktop-automation/runner.test.mjs
- npm run sync:local
- node scripts/reload-local-obsidian-plugin.mjs
- npm run test:native:desktop:chatview-stress
- npm run test:native:desktop:stress
- node scripts/release-plugin.mjs --dry-run --version 5.3.0
  --allow-dirty --notes-file docs/release-notes/5.3.0.md

Adds pnpm override for node-forge (via @unlighthouse/cli → listhen)
to fix signature forgery, DoS, and certificate bypass vulnerabilities.

- Pin node-forge override to exact version 1.4.0

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* perf(mobile-storefront): migrate FlatList to FlashList v2 across 8 screens

Replace FlatList with @shopify/flash-list (v2) in all eligible list screens
for better virtualization and recycling performance. FlashList v2 auto-measures
items, so no estimatedItemSize needed. Also removes FlatList-specific perf
hacks (removeClippedSubviews, maxToRenderPerBatch, windowSize, getItemLayout,
columnWrapperStyle) that FlashList handles internally.

Migrated: category, search, orders, notifications, receipts, saved (tab + page),
SearchOverlay.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* perf(mobile): upgrade @shopify/flash-list 2.0.2 → 2.3.1 and migrate ChatModal

- Bump flash-list to 2.3.1 in both mobile-storefront and mobile-admin
- Migrate ChatModal from FlatList to FlashList (now possible with v2.3.1's
  inverted prop support and FlashListRef type)
- Update use-chat.ts ref type from FlatList to FlashListRef<ChatMessage>

commit.message:
COE-312: Add session notes, outcome states, and artifact registry (#15)

* feat(session,artifact): add session notes, outcome states, and artifact registry

Summary:
- Add SessionOutcomeState enum (valid, invalid, aborted) to domain models
- Add notes and outcome_state fields to Session domain model and ORM
- Add experiment_id field to Artifact model for experiment-scoped artifacts
- Create database migration 520517cac40b for schema changes
- Update SessionService with outcome state handling and notes support
- Create ArtifactRegistryService for artifact management
- Add ArtifactRepository interface for artifact persistence
- Update session CLI with --notes option on create, --outcome on finalize
- Add session add-notes CLI command for updating notes
- Create artifact CLI commands: register, list, show, remove
- Link artifacts to sessions or experiments (at least one required)

Rationale:
- Operators need to document session context with notes during/after runs
- Outcome states allow marking sessions as invalid/aborted while keeping them
  visible for audit (they can be excluded from comparisons)
- Artifact registry enables attaching exports/reports to sessions or experiments
  for traceability and comparison workflows

Tests:
- make test: 77 tests passed
- make type-check: passed

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

- Add tests for SQLArtifactRepository (7 tests)
- Add tests for update_session_notes service method (4 tests)
- Fix UUID slicing in artifact list command
- Add ArtifactRegistryService to exports

All 235 tests passing

- Removed ArtifactRegistryService from services_abc.py

- All 236 tests passing

commit.message:
fix(mobile-storefront): fix Colors type import in product-card types (#941)

Use `import type Colors` with `(typeof Colors)['light']` to correctly
reference the color scheme type. The previous `import type { default as Colors }`
with `Colors['light']` caused TS2749 since TypeScript treated Colors as a value.

commit.message:
🎨 Palette: [UX improvement] Improve PhoneInput accessibility (#934)

* 🎨 Palette: [UX improvement] Improve PhoneInput accessibility

* What: Added proper accessibility attributes to the country selector modal and touchables in PhoneInput component.
* Why: Complex React Native custom form controls with decorative elements cause screen reader noise by individually reading them. Grouping elements inside TouchableOpacity with specific roles and hints improves UX.
* Accessibility:
  - Added accessibilityRole="button", accessibilityLabel, accessibilityHint, and activeOpacity={0.7} to the country selector button.
  - Added accessibilityRole="header" to the modal header text.
  - Hid decorative icons, flags, and duplicated text inside the country button and country row touchables using accessibilityElementsHidden={true} and importantForAccessibility="no".
  - Added accessibilityState={{ selected: true/false }} to country row options.
* Impact: Enhances accessibility for screen reader users selecting country codes within the mobile storefront app.

Co-authored-by: ogabasseyy <196925138+ogabasseyy@users.noreply.github.com>

* style(mobile-storefront): fix indentation in PhoneInput accessibility props

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:

Co-Authored-By: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

fix(jumia): await refetch before URL replace on connect (#937)

* fix(jumia): await refetch before URL replace on successful connect

refetch() was called without await, then router.replace() fired
immediately — the re-render could interrupt the data fetch, leaving
the page showing stale (disconnected) state.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(jumia): use static import for logo to bypass [slug] route

The /jumia-logo.png public path was being caught by the [slug]
dynamic route on production, returning a 404 HTML page instead of
the image. Using a static import serves it from _next/static instead.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(jumia): auto-sync orders for newly connected shops

After OAuth connect, the callback now includes shop IDs in the
redirect URL. The channels page reads these IDs and automatically
triggers order sync only for the newly connected shops, avoiding
unnecessary re-sync of pre-existing integrations.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(jumia): use public logo path in channels page

* fix(channels): address review — await refetch before replace, fix mock type

- Move router.replace inside refetch().then() to prevent stale state
- Filter shopIds to only active integrations in callback route
- Fix mockRefetch to resolve [] instead of undefined

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(jumia): scope auto-sync to newly connected shops

repository.name: Baci
repository.description: Experiment

commit.message:
feat(migrations): stream import job progress via Supabase Realtime (#935)

* fix(jumia): relax token schema to match Vendor Center API response

The JumiaTokenResponseSchema was too strict — it required exact `Bearer`/`N_A`
enum for token_type and strict number types for expires_in fields. Jumia's
Vendor Center API may return lowercase token_type or string-encoded numbers,
causing Zod validation to reject valid token responses during OAuth callback.

Also improves error logging in the callback catch-all to include structured
error details instead of raw error object.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(jumia): harden token schema and callback error logging

- Replace z.coerce.number() with union(number, numeric-string) to reject
  booleans/arrays that coerce silently
- Add .trim() to all string validators to reject whitespace-only values
- Normalize non-Error throwables in callback catch-all to a safe shape
  instead of logging raw thrown objects
- Add negative-path tests: empty/null token_type, non-numeric strings,
  boolean/array expires_in

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(jumia): add request throttling to respect Vendor Center rate limits

Jumia enforces 200 req/min and 4 req/sec at MasterShop level. Without
throttling, paginated getAll*() calls burst through this limit during
product imports or order syncs for busy merchants.

- Add 250ms minimum interval between requests in JumiaClient.request()
- Add 250ms delay between pages in edge function order sync loop
- Add throttle test verifying sequential requests are spaced correctly

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(jumia): add diagnostic error codes to OAuth callback for root cause analysis

Token exchange failures now return `?error=token_exchange_failed` instead of
generic `connection_failed`, with detailed logging of the redirect URI and
HTTP status. Also adds user-friendly error messages for the new codes and
aligns connect route to use `getAppUrl()` for redirect URI consistency.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(jumia): normalize oauth callback redirect uri

* fix(jumia): serialize request throttling

* fix(jumia): tighten request gate release handling

* fix(jumia): address oauth review findings

* fix(jumia): tighten callback and refresh handling

* fix(jumia): harden callback config and client throttling

* fix(deps): override brace-expansion and happy-dom to resolve security alerts

- brace-expansion ^5.0.2 → ^5.0.5 (DoS via zero-step sequence)
- happy-dom >=20.8.8 (unsanitized export names → code execution)

Resolves all 6 open Dependabot alerts.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(jumia): guard oauth redirects with app url

* fix(jumia): require explicit oauth redirect uri

* fix(jumia): log token exchange diagnostics

* fix(jumia): harden error detail sanitization

* feat(migrations): stream import job progress via Supabase Realtime

Replace 1s polling with Supabase Realtime subscription on import_jobs
for instant progress updates, with a 5s polling fallback for resilience.

- Add adaptive progress reporter cadence (~20 writes/job vs fixed 25-row)
- Enable Realtime on import_jobs table (idempotent migration)
- Show indeterminate progress bar during initial data loading phase
- Handle terminal transitions (validating → preview_ready) with row fetch
- Invalidate in-flight polls when Realtime updates arrive
- Add reduced-motion support for progress animation

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(migrations): address CodeRabbit review findings

- Add background: true to terminal transition refreshJob call
- Handle Realtime subscribe errors (CHANNEL_ERROR/TIMED_OUT)
- Assert aria-valuenow absent for indeterminate progress
- Tighten adaptive write count bounds (20-27 instead of 10-35)
- Add tests for subscribe error handling and background flag

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(migrations): wire Realtime callbacks into migration jobs hook

Connect onRealtimeJobUpdate and onRefreshRequestIdBump from
useMigrationJobs into useMigrationJobPolling so Realtime payloads
merge into the jobs list via mergeJobs and invalidate stale polls.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(migrations): address stale ref, aria-valuenow, test perf, and migration guard

- Terminal transition uses includeJob: true so refreshJob reads the fresh
  server status instead of stale selectedJobRef (race condition fix)
- Forward value prop to ProgressPrimitive.Root so Radix sets aria-valuenow
  on determinate progress bars and omits it for indeterminate
- Replace 5823-row iteration with boundary-based calls in adaptive step
  test (exact 26 writes assertion, much faster)
- Guard migration against missing supabase_realtime publication so it's
  safe in environments without Realtime

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(tests): use exact counts and monotonic sequences in progress reporter tests

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix(virtual-camera): stabilize demand lifecycle and release flow

Summary:
- gate host preview on real capture demand so the menubar app stays idle
  without an open window or external virtual camera consumer
- move early wake-up to authorizedToStartStream, add demand inspection and
  Chromium/native probe harnesses, and harden the background test scripts
- add local install and DMG publishing skills, bump the app and sysext to
  0.1.0 (10), and refresh the manual release asset workflow

Rationale:
- the host was keeping the physical camera alive after UI teardown because
  preview startup and shutdown were still coupled to appearance state
- the sysext wake-up path was too noisy and a stale extension process hid
  the real behavior until the bundle version forced a clean restart
- reproducible local probes and a repeatable DMG flow are needed so the
  fix can be validated and shipped without manual QA loops

Tests:
- xcodebuild -project Reframe.xcodeproj -scheme Reframe -destination
  'platform=macOS' -derivedDataPath build/test test
- ./.agents/skills/local-test/scripts/build_and_install.sh
- build/cli/Build/Products/Release/reframe print-demand
- CLI_PATH="$PWD/build/cli/Build/Products/Release/reframe" APP_PATH='/Applications/Reframe.app' ./Scripts/run-chromium-background-virtual-camera-probe.sh
- ./.agents/skills/update-dmg/scripts/build_release_dmg.sh /Applications/Reframe.app build/Reframe-0.1.0.dmg
- ./.agents/skills/update-dmg/scripts/publish_release_asset.sh v0.1.0 build/Reframe-0.1.0.dmg

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>
